name: Netskope SASE
description: Netskope Intelligent SSE as the primary SWG/CASB/DLP layer. A Netskope-first stack common in organizations that have standardized on Netskope's Security Service Edge platform for cloud security. Detection relies on Netskope's native AI app discovery and generic network rules.
compensating_controls: Netskope's Cloud Confidence Index (CCI) provides native AI application discovery and risk scoring (DET-NS-001).
Netskope inline DLP can inspect content uploaded to AI services for sensitive data patterns.
Netskope's real-time protection policies can block, coach, or alert on AI app access with granular activity-level controls.
For endpoint detection: pair with your existing EDR (Defender, CrowdStrike, SentinelOne) and export events to your SIEM.
Identity-side: pair with your identity provider's conditional-access policies for device-trust enforcement.

things_to_know.title: Netskope native AI app tagging
things_to_know.content: Netskope's app catalog tracks Generative AI apps natively with CCI ratings. Use the built-in Generative AI tag for immediate discovery without custom configuration.

things_to_know.title: Steering configuration is critical
things_to_know.content: Ensure the Netskope Client is deployed to all managed endpoints with proper steering configuration. Without client coverage, users bypassing the proxy are invisible.

things_to_know.title: No endpoint-level detection
things_to_know.content: Without a dedicated EDR integration, this stack cannot detect AI desktop applications or browser extensions at the endpoint. Pair with an EDR for full coverage.

things_to_know.title: SIEM integration recommended
things_to_know.content: Export Netskope events via Cloud Exchange to your SIEM for correlation with identity and endpoint data sources.

policy_token_replacements.siem_tool: your central SIEM (via Netskope Cloud Exchange export)
policy_token_replacements.edr_tool: your endpoint detection tool (pair with existing EDR)
